mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive return frequency drops below 55% during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 9.0 percent, consumer spending collapses and corporate earnings forecasts are downgraded, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in greater than 60 percent of instances where UNRATE exceeds 9.0 percent for three consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate synchronized consumer and business confidence, generating positive equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative returns in greater than 55 percent of cases when both series rise month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a post-crisis recovery phase signals capacity utilization improvements and margin expansion, driving positive short-term S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to predict positive 5-day returns when CPIAUCSL growth exceeds 3.0 percent annually.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month gains in total nonfarm payrolls increase aggregate income and corporate revenue expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Payroll increases correlate with negative 20-day returns in greater than 65 percent of observations over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index surpasses 220.0, inflation expectations force monetary tightening and compress equity valuations, resulting in negative forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 60-day returns in greater than 60 percent of periods following CPIAUCSL breaches of 220.0.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between declining real GDP growth and falling retail sales confirms a broad demand contraction, triggering negative equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The divergence signal yields positive returns in greater than 55 percent of cases when both series decline simultaneously.

mechanism_narrative: In a zero-lower-bound environment, stable industrial output combined with accommodative policy signals liquidity absorption and risk-on positioning, driving positive 5-day S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in greater than 60 percent of instances when FEDFUNDS remains below 0.5 percent for consecutive months.
